rationale: Synthetic det_clone_up_to_view_equal demands r1.id == r2.id (i.e. SMT/structural equality on Vec<u8>). The only available hypothesis is r1@ == self_@ && r2@ == self_@, which (via EndPoint.view = AbstractEndPoint{id: self.id@}) yields only r1.id@ == r2.id@ (i.e. Seq<u8> equality of the views). Verus's vstd has no axiom equating Vec<u8> SMT equality to Seq view equality (confirmed by minimal repro: `requires a.id@ == b.id@ ensures a.id == b.id` fails to verify, even with `broadcast use vstd::std_specs::vec::group_vec_axioms`). The underlying clone_up_to_view's spec is only `res@ == self@`, so the synthetic check (policy errs_equivalent=True, opaque_ok=False) is strictly stronger than the spec and cannot be discharged without an assume/admit/external_body, all of which are forbidden. Left the best-effort proof block (cases on the premise and chains the view equalities r1.id@ == self_.id@ == r2.id@) so that the structural derivation up to the unprovable Vec-level step is documented.
